endpoints.name: Alola Island Kahunas (Hala, Olivia, Nanu, Hapu)
9-node `Like [[...]]` faction-collective character-layer precedent peer-listing (Caveats section, modeled-as bullet)

description: ONE snip. Dissolved 5 of 9 schema-precedent wikilinks at alola-kahunas.md line 89 — converted [[team-rocket]], [[aether-foundation]], [[endriders]], [[orzhov-syndicate]], [[azorius-senate]] to backticks per bbl-wikilink-vs-backtick-discipline. The 5 wikilinks express pure 'modeled-as schema-precedent peer' relations — Alola-Kahunas has no canonical narrative tissue with Team Rocket, Aether Foundation, Endriders (Yu-Gi-Oh!), Orzhov Syndicate (MTG-Ravnica), or Azorius Senate (MTG-Ravnica). PRESERVED 4 wikilinks that DO have real-thematic-edge presence in this file's ## See also section: [[alola-elite-four]] (L93+L95 — Olivia/Hala cross-recruitment dual-membership), [[alola-trial-captains]] (L94+L95 — three-tier Alola progression sibling tier), [[galar-gym-challenge]] (L97 — opposing-ideology Tapu-sanction-vs-corporate-televised-athletics contrast), and [[team-skull]] (L95+L96 — Guzma & Hala TAG TEAM narrative bridge, the canonical failed-Kahuna-trial-attempter narrative inverse). Triple-justification: (a) DIRECT FOLLOW-UP TO WAVE-130 SNIP-PRECEDENT — staged as top next-pass candidate in reports/edges_pending/wave130-snip-zendikari-resistance-schema-precedent-peerlist.json; same heuristic, same shape, next-strongest corpus target. The wave-130 sidecar predicted 3 preserves; corpus-walking caught a 4th (team-skull's real-thematic Guzma-Hala bridge). The PRESERVE_WIKILINKS_IF_THEMATIC_ELSEWHERE_IN_FILE guard surfaced one more preserved link than the staged prediction — Edgelord's manual case-by-case discipline is the value-add over a future mechanical janny sweep, (b) NO REAL-THEMATIC LOSS — the 5 snipped wikilinks ([[team-rocket]], [[aether-foundation]], [[endriders]], [[orzhov-syndicate]], [[azorius-senate]]) appear ONLY at L89 in this file with zero real-thematic-edge presence anywhere else (verified via grep). The bullet's full structural-template-citation prose is retained ('per the Endriders precedent', 'the character layer accepts both individual named characters and collective institutional entities', 'Single character layer, same precedent') — the Endriders precedent citation survives as a plain capitalized reference within the prose, with the wikilink dropped because schema-precedent citation is exactly the backtick-zone, (c) GRAPH-VIEW NOISE REDUCTION — 5 cross-franchise false edges dissolved from alola-kahunas pointing at unrelated faction-collective nodes (Pokemon Team Rocket / Pokemon Aether Foundation / Yu-Gi-Oh Endriders / MTG Orzhov / MTG Azorius). The mobile graph view that surfaced this proliferation in wave 71 (nicol-bolas false-edges) gets cleaner; the four PRESERVED wikilinks (all real-thematic Pokemon-canon-adjacent nodes) carry the actual narrative edge load. HTML comment marker added inline to flag the dissolution for future passes and reference the wave-130 precedent.

The 'multi-triangle meta-cycle' framing IS a Potion-rule-sub-rule-4 trigger (parent's brief flagged it correctly), but the dissolution would be a multi-line body-prose softening + H1 retitle + frontmatter `name:` and `faction:` field rewrite — that's a Dark Nodesley EX body-softening mission, not an Edgelord edge snip. Staging separately: a DARK NODESLEY EX dispatch could rewrite the framing as 'BFZ + OGW two-triangle adversary-coupling node' or similar standard-graph-theory phrasing while preserving the actual canonical finding (Wizards designed two thesis-axis triangles in sequence). NOT this pass.

Extend reports/_scratch/janny_bolas_schema_precedent_sweep.py with PRESERVE_IF_RECURS_IN_SAME_FILE_AS_REAL_THEMATIC guard. Mechanical sweep without the guard would clobber real-thematic edges; with the guard it can process the remaining ~10-12 corpus character nodes in one pass.

Sweep candidate: grep for 'Modeled as a character-layer node, not a hub' + 'Like [[' across cards/_characters/ to enumerate remaining schema-precedent peer-listings. The two waves so far (130 + 131) covered the two longest peer-lists in corpus; the tail is shorter peer-lists per character-node.
edgelord_notes: Mood enum `enlightened-corrective` — direct continuation of wave-130 corrective discipline. Refused dual-dispatch (one cut per invocation holds; the body-prose Potion-rule-sub-rule-4 mission is mode-separate and surfaced as next-pass DARK NODESLEY EX candidate). The corpus-walk caught the team-skull preservation case the wave-130 staged prediction missed — that's the manual case-by-case Edgelord discipline doing what a mechanical sweep could not, and the lesson is captured in the next-pass-suggestion to add the PRESERVE_IF_RECURS_IN_SAME_FILE guard before any janny extension ships. HTML comment marker added inline on line 89 references this sidecar and the wave-130 precedent so future passes can trace the dissolution lineage.
